methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $5M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Robotics & Manufacturing (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 11 same-sector peers exist in Robotics & Manufacturing, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
Corvex Systems is a seed-stage robotics and manufacturing company that has raised $5M+. The valuation analysis draws from a broader comp universe of 9 private peers (including 1X Technologies, Divergent, Exotec, Locus Robotics, and Bedrock Robotics) and 1 public comparable.

Stage-progression methodology is applicable and represents the primary valuation anchor. Using total raised ($5M) as a proxy baseline and applying a typical seed-to-next-stage step-up multiplier of 2.5x yields an implied valuation of $12M. Peer-median analysis was not feasible; while 11 robotics and manufacturing companies exist in the comp universe, none met the narrow criteria for same-stage, adjacent-stage, or similar-tier peers (requiring a minimum of 3 datapoints). A broad sector median would introduce unacceptable noise given the disparity in stage and scale across the Robotics & Manufacturing landscape.

The triangulation supports a valuation range of $11M to $14M, with a median point estimate of $12M. This range reflects the single applicable method and the limited narrow peer visibility at the seed stage. Key uncertainties include the absence of directly comparable seed-stage robotics valuations and limited visibility into the company's product maturity, unit economics, or near-term revenue trajectory. A refresh of this analysis at Series A would benefit from tighter peer comparability and additional valuation methods.
